key_themes: NASDAQ delisting to OTC (ITRMF) with leadership resignation
Exhausted cash runway, failed capital raises, and unsuccessful strategic sales
Modest sales vs. high costs of commercializing FDA-approved ORLYNVAH (sulopenem)
Near-total shareholder value destruction with negligible expected recovery
summary: Iterum Therapeutics (ITRM/ITRMF) filed a winding-up petition in Ireland on March 27, 2026, leading to the appointment of provisional liquidators from Teneo. The move was driven by critically low cash, inability to raise capital or sell assets after an EGM constrained the board, weak commercialization of its approved oral antibiotic, and failure to secure buyers despite discussions with two parties. The company was delisted from NASDAQ effective April 1, 2026; executives resigned, and the business is in an orderly wind-down that will likely include market withdrawal of ORLYNVAH, leaving shareholders with virtually nothing. Investor discussion is sparse and deeply negative, dominated by acknowledgment of collapse with only isolated gambling on extreme volatility in the OTC shares now trading near $0.02–0.03.[[1]](https://ir.iterumtx.com/press-releases/detail/165/iterum-therapeutics-announces-filing-of-winding-up-petition)[[2]](https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/iterum-files-wind-up-petition-due-limited-cash-inability-raise-equity-2026-03-27/)[[3]](https://www.fiercepharma.com/pharma/iterum-initiates-wind-down-after-failure-offload-antibiotic-sluggish-sales)
sources: 8+ news articles / PRs (company IR, Reuters, FiercePharma, GlobeNewswire, SEC 8-K) on winding-up filing, X: ~15-20 relevant recent posts (mostly delisting, liquidation, and penny-stock volatility), StockTwits ITRM community (active monitoring of collapsing penny stock), Limited recent Reddit threads (older hype vs. current liquidation reality)
